message:
fix(http): byte-level header parsing to prevent UTF-8 assumption exploits

Rewrite parse_header_line() to operate on raw bytes with explicit
UTF-8 validation at the boundaries instead of assuming the entire
line is valid UTF-8 upfront.  The previous string-based approach
used str::find(':') and str::trim(), which could behave unexpectedly
on malformed input containing multi-byte sequences that happen to
include the colon byte.

Key changes:
  - Split on first b':' in byte slice, validate name/value as UTF-8
    separately via std::str::from_utf8()
  - Check for leading/trailing whitespace in raw_name bytes directly
    instead of comparing trimmed vs untrimmed string slices
  - Manual byte-level whitespace trimming for header values

Also rewrites decode_head() to use byte window scanning (b"\r\n")
instead of str::split("\r\n"), eliminating an early UTF-8 conversion
that would reject headers with valid HTTP/1.1 but non-UTF-8 bytes
in values.  Pre-allocates headers Vec with capacity 8 to avoid
repeated reallocations on typical request sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
